shortcomings: No explicit frontend web development experience or evidence of HTML/CSS/JavaScript proficiency (the resume lists backend and ML focus but does not mention HTML, CSS, or JS).
Frontend framework experience is absent (no React, Angular, Vue, or TypeScript listed) despite the role emphasizing building responsive, user-friendly websites.

Lack of explicit demonstration of building user-facing UI or collaborating with design teams on UI/UX; the resume shows cross-functional collaboration but no UI/UX-focused outcomes or design-led development.

No clear examples of integrating backend APIs with a frontend or handling data exchange in a web client context; most API references are to serverless/background services (API Gateway, Lambda) rather than frontend-backend integration.
Frontend performance, accessibility, responsive design, and cross-browser compatibility concepts are not mentioned, yet they are key for the role.

Absence of explicit documentation or maintainable frontend code practices (e.g., frontend testing, code reviews, documentation for UI components) in the resume; the emphasis is on ML pipelines and backend infrastructure.
No explicit remote/contractor-web-development experience highlighted; current roles are backend/ML-centric and may not align with typical Web Developer contract expectations.
Missing a targeted summary/objective and a dedicated section highlighting web development outcomes or portfolio items relevant to the job description.
missing_keywords: REST/HTTP API integration from the frontend
experience_gaps: No demonstrated frontend development work (HTML/CSS/JS) or frontend frameworks; resume shows backend/API and ML work but not frontend deliverables.
No evidence of building or shipping user-facing websites or dashboards, only backend services and ML platforms.
No explicit mention of collaborating with design teams on UI/UX or producing design-led front-end outcomes; mentions of "cross-functional teams" but not UI design collaboration.

Front-end related performance, accessibility, and responsive design metrics are missing; the candidate lacks frontend-focused impact measurements (e.g., page load times, accessibility conformance, responsive behavior).
weak_sections: TECHNICAL SKILLS emphasize ML frameworks (LangChain, RAG, HuggingFace, TensorFlow, PyTorch) and backend tools; no HTML/CSS/JS or frontend frameworks listed.
PROJECT EXPERIENCE and WORK EXPERIENCE sections focus on AI, ML evaluation, and backend cloud architectures (AWS Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB) with little to no frontend/web UI context.
Education and coursework are ML/data-centric; lacks a clear, frontend-relevant narrative or examples that would reassure a client seeking Web Developer capabilities.
overall_assessment: The candidate is strong in backend engineering and AI/ML systems with solid project-based impact metrics. However, the resume does not demonstrate core Web Developer competencies needed for this remote frontend/Full-Stack role: explicit HTML/CSS/JavaScript proficiency, frontend frameworks, UI/UX collaboration, frontend API integration, and frontend-quality practices. To improve fit for this job, the candidate should add concrete frontend experience, highlight any web UI or dashboard work, include frontend-related keywords, and provide examples or a portfolio link showcasing responsive, user-focused web interfaces.
